title: The Layby Where Journeys Pause

features.name: parked-vehicles
features.desc-on-touch: Cars and trucks settle into the red gravel like they've finally admitted they were tired. Their engines tick as they cool. Each one carries a story that ends here, at least for now.

features.name: rest-shelter
features.desc-on-touch: Corrugated iron roof, bench seats, a small table someone carved initials into. The shade here holds the stories of a thousand brief conversations.

features.name: thermos-circle
features.desc-on-touch: Coolers and thermoses arranged on the table like people waiting their turn. Someone always leaves coffee. Someone always drinks it. The exchange never requires words.

features.name: road-markings
features.desc-on-touch: White paint, weathered to pale grey. Here, the journey's rhythm is still visible: broken lines, direction arrows, the ghost of where people decided to stop and breathe.

features.name: travellers-log
features.desc-on-touch: Napkins, envelopes, margins of old newspapers. People write their names here, or their destinations, or just 'made it this far.' The archive has filed this under both evidence and prayer.

inscription: here the road remembers why it was built: not to carry traffic, but to give it permission to rest

file.name: Layby_Napkins_Collected_1982_2001.txt
file.note: Transcriptions of what travellers wrote when they stopped. The archive found it filed under both local history and belonging.

exits.label: back toward the terminal where parking dreams
deeper into paddock country, where highways become rumours
exits.to: NEW: where traffic's engine-sound becomes wind between grass

exits.label: following the asphalt north into older routes
